18.4     This paragraph applies to service of a DAPO varied or discharged by the court without notice as it applies to service of a DAPO made by the court without notice.


## 19. Service of DAPO made in existing proceedings


19.1     This paragraph applies where a DAPO is made by the court—


(a)        on application under paragraph 6 by the person to be protected in existing proceedings in which both the person and P are parties; or


(b)        of its own initiative under paragraph 16.


19.2     Service must be—


(a)        as soon as reasonably practicable; and


(b)        in accordance with the relevant provisions of paragraph 17.


19.3     Where paragraph 19.1(a) applies, the person to be protected must serve—


(a)        a copy of the DAPO; and


(b)        where the DAPO is made without notice to P, a copy of the application for the DAPO together with supporting statement, if made in writing,


on P, personally, and on any other person named in the order.


19.4     Where the DAPO relates to a property and details of any mortgagee or landlord of the property were set out in the DAPO application pursuant to paragraph 3.4(c)(ii), the person to be protected must serve a copy of the DAPO on the mortgagee or landlord of the property.


19.5     Where paragraph 19.1(b) applies, a copy of the DAPO must be served—


(a)        on the persons the court has ordered under paragraph 16.2; and


(b)        in accordance with paragraphs 16.3 and 16.4.


19.6     This paragraph applies to service of a DAPO varied or discharged by the court in existing proceedings as it applies to service of a DAPO made by the court in existing proceedings.


## VIII — NOTIFICATION TO THE POLICE


## 20. Notifying the police of a DAPO


20.1     Where the court makes a DAPO, notification must be given to the police force for P’s address by the court officer emailing a copy of the order to the email address for that police force.


20.2     The email notification must be sent within 1 day of whichever is the earlier of—


(a)        the DAPO being sealed; or
